content.full_text:
June 9, 1976


Mr. Howard P. Abelson
Deputy City Attorney
City of Oakland - City Hall
14th and Washington Streets
Oakland, California 94612

Dear Mr. Abelson:

     This is in response to your letter dated May 21, 1976,
and will serve to confirm the substance of our telephone
conversation of May 20, 1976.

     As I indicated to you then, Mr. Vogley's appearance
before any public agency on behalf of a client would create
a question relative to whether he was attempting to use
his official position to influence a governmental decision
in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest". Government Code §87100. By regulation, 2 Cal.
Admin. Code §13700(e), the Commission has defined "in any way
attempting to use his or her official position to influence
a governmental decision" to include:

     ".... furthering or attempting to affect in any
manner any decision:

          (1) Within or before his or her agency; or

          (2) Before any agency which is appointed by or
subject to the budgetary control of his or her
agency."

     Since the City Council clearly is not this type of agency in
the case of Planning Commissioner Vogley, the regulation does
not bar Mr. Vogley's participation in the manner specified in
your letter.

     I trust this answers your question. Thank you again for
your interest in the Political Reform Act.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Staff Attorney
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division

/glb

CITY OF OAKLAND

CITY HALL · 14TH AND WASHINGTON STREETS · OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612

Office of the City Attorney
David A. Self
City Attorney

May 21, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
SACRAMENTO CA 95814

Attention: Mr. Michael Bennett, Executive Director

Re: Request for Opinion 76056

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Thank you very much for your letter of May 7, 1976,
responding to our request for opinion. While your
letter specifically responds to the question posed in
our request for opinion dated April 20, 1976, there is
still a further question we have regarding this matter.

That question is, assuming the same fact situation,
may Mr. Vouleu appear before the City Council of the
City of Oakland in his capacity as landscape architect
and land use planner of the project in question.

We would appreciate it very much if this additional
question were treated in the same manner as our original
request for opinion.

Very truly yours,

DAVID A. SELF
City Attorney

HOWARD P. ABELSON
Deputy City Attorney

HPA:lf

[The image shows a very faint or heavily faded document. Most of the text is not legible enough to transcribe with confidence. There appears to be a logo or emblem in the upper left corner, and some text that might read "CASINO OVED" or similar near the top, but the majority of the document content is too faint or illegible to accurately transcribe.]
